description: Frequency ID — first interactive ear training exercise. User listens to EQ'd audio, turns SSL-style rotary knobs (frequency, gain, Q) to guess the applied EQ. 5-round game loop with difficulty selection, A/B toggle, and localStorage persistence.

userStories.title: Core game engine — types, state machine, scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed game engine with a state machine, round tracking, and scoring so all future exercises can build on the same foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/game/types.ts exports: Difficulty ('easy' | 'medium' | 'hard'), GameState ('idle' | 'selecting_difficulty' | 'round_active' | 'round_result' | 'game_over'), Round, GameSession, GameResult
GameSession tracks: difficulty, rounds (array of 5 Round), currentRoundIndex, score, startedAt (ISO string), endedAt (ISO string | null)
Round tracks: targetFreq, targetGainDb, targetQ, userFreq (number | null), userGainDb (number | null), userQ (number | null), result: 'correct' | 'incorrect' | 'pending', errorMarginPct

src/lib/game/config.ts exports DIFFICULTY_CONFIG: { easy: { gainRange: 3, qMin: 0.5, qMax: 1, errorMargin: 0.15 }, medium: { gainRange: 5, qMin: 0.5, qMax: 3, errorMargin: 0.10 }, hard: { gainRange: 10, qMin: 0.5, qMax: 10, errorMargin: 0.05 } }
src/lib/game/engine.ts exports createGameSession(difficulty: Difficulty): GameSession — randomly generates 5 rounds using FR-2/FR-3/FR-4 formulas from the PRD
src/lib/game/engine.ts exports evaluateGuess(round: Round, userFreq: number): 'correct' | 'incorrect' — uses FR-5: Math.abs(userFreq - targetFreq) / targetFreq <= errorMargin
FR-2 target frequency formula: f = Math.round(80 * Math.pow(10000 / 80, Math.random())) — log-space 80Hz–10kHz
FR-3 target gain formula: gainRange * Math.random() * (Math.random() > 0.5 ? 1 : -1), clamped so |gainDb| >= 1
FR-4 target Q formula: linear random in [qMin, qMax] for the difficulty
Scoring: 1 point per correct frequency guess; max 5 points per game
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)

userStories.title: Route scaffold + difficulty selection screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate to /games/frequency-id and pick a difficulty before playing so I can control the challenge level.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: src/routes/games/frequency-id/+page.svelte exists
Page imports createGameSession from src/lib/game/engine.ts and DIFFICULTY_CONFIG from src/lib/game/config.ts
Page uses $state for gameState (GameState) and session (GameSession | null)
Difficulty selection screen renders when gameState is 'idle' or 'selecting_difficulty'
Three DifficultyCard components (or equivalent): Easy / Medium / Hard, each showing gain range (e.g. '±3 dB'), error margin (e.g. '15% tolerance'), Q range (e.g. 'Q 0.5–1')
Clicking a difficulty calls createGameSession(difficulty) and sets gameState to 'round_active'
Back link (← Back) navigates to /
Read tasks/design-frequency-id.md section on DifficultySelect screen before implementing
Monochrome, minimalist — consistent with dashboard aesthetic; use bg-card, border-border, text-foreground tokens
Use Svelte MCP (list-sections → get-documentation) before writing any .svelte file

userStories.title: Web Audio API engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an audio engine that loads an audio sample, applies a BiquadFilter for the B signal, and allows toggling between flat (A) and filtered (B) playback.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/audio/frequency-id-engine.ts exports class FrequencyIdEngine
load(url: string): Promise<void> — fetches and decodes audio via AudioContext.decodeAudioData
play(mode: 'A' | 'B'): void — creates a new AudioBufferSourceNode (one-shot, per FR-11), sets loop=true, connects to the appropriate graph branch and starts playback
stop(): void — stops and disconnects the current source node
setFilter(freq: number, gainDb: number, q: number): void — updates BiquadFilterNode params in real-time (frequency.value, gain.value, Q.value)
setMode(mode: 'A' | 'B'): void — switches between flat (A, filter bypassed via disconnect/reconnect per FR-1) and filtered (B) without restarting audio
BiquadFilterNode type is 'peaking'
A mode: source → destination (filter node not in path); B mode: source → filter → destination
Audio loops continuously (loop = true)
SSR-safe: AudioContext only instantiated in browser environments (guard with typeof window !== 'undefined')
Placeholder audio file at static/audio/freq-id-placeholder.wav (create a minimal valid WAV file — 1 second of silence, 44100 Hz, 16-bit mono)
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)

userStories.title: Rotary knob component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to turn rotary knobs (SSL strip style) to set my frequency, gain, and Q guesses with an intuitive drag interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/components/RotaryKnob.svelte exists
Props: value: number, min: number, max: number, label: string, unit: string, logarithmic?: boolean (default false), onchange: (v: number) => void, disabled?: boolean (default false)
Visual: circle ~44px diameter (w-11 h-11), monochrome — bg-muted border border-border rounded-full
Notch indicator: small line/dot on the rim at the current rotation angle, pointing from center outward
Rotation maps value to angle: -135° (min) to +135° (max); logarithmic mapping when logarithmic=true
Drag interaction: pointerdown captures pointer (setPointerCapture), pointermove on the element tracks vertical delta; drag up increases value, drag down decreases; pointerup releases
Value label displayed below knob: formatted as '1.2 kHz' (freq), '-5 dB' (gain), '2.4' (Q, no unit suffix)
Label text: text-xs font-mono text-muted-foreground
Keyboard: focus on knob div (tabindex=0), ArrowUp/ArrowDown adjust by 1 step, Shift+Arrow by 10 steps
When disabled=true: opacity-50 pointer-events-none, no drag/keyboard response
Read tasks/design-frequency-id.md RotaryKnob section before implementing
Use Svelte MCP (list-sections → get-documentation) before writing any .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on every .svelte file and fix all reported issues
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)
Lint passes (pnpm lint)
Verify in browser: navigate to /games/frequency-id, select difficulty, check knobs render and respond to drag

userStories.title: Frequency strip display
userStories.description: As a user, I want a wide vertical frequency strip showing 20Hz–20kHz with a marker at my selected frequency so I have spatial reference while sweeping.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/components/FrequencyStrip.svelte exists
Props: value: number (Hz, user marker), targetValue?: number (Hz, correct freq shown on round result), showTarget?: boolean (default false)
Container: w-[120px] h-full flex flex-row — two columns: left label gutter (w-[80px]) + right bar (w-[40px])

Label gutter: relative positioned; tick labels at 20, 63, 125, 250, 500, 1k, 2k, 4k, 8k, 16k, 20k Hz — each label is absolute right-1 text-[9px] font-mono text-muted-foreground leading-none, vertically positioned matching the tick
Bar column: bg-card border-l border-border relative — tick marks are absolute w-full border-t border-border/40 lines at each frequency position
Log position formula: pct = Math.log(value / 20) / Math.log(20000 / 20); top = (1 - pct) * 100%
User marker: absolute left-0 right-0 border-t-2 border-foreground/60, positioned via style top={...}%; transitions with transition-[top] duration-150
Target marker (showTarget=true): absolute left-0 right-0 border-t-2 border-primary, no transition (instantaneous reveal)
Mobile responsive: label gutter has class 'hidden sm:flex' (hidden on mobile, visible on sm+); container collapses to w-10 on mobile
Read tasks/design-frequency-id.md FrequencyStrip section before implementing
Use Svelte MCP (list-sections → get-documentation) before writing any .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on every .svelte file and fix all reported issues
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)
Lint passes (pnpm lint)
Verify in browser: navigate to /games/frequency-id, select difficulty — frequency strip shows with tick labels and moving marker

userStories.title: Game round UI — knobs, A/B switch, submit
userStories.description: As a user, I want the main exercise UI with the frequency strip, three knobs, A/B toggle, and submit button during a round so I can make and submit my guess.
acceptanceCriteria: Round UI renders when gameState === 'round_active'
Round counter displayed: 'Round N / 5' using currentRoundIndex + 1
Layout: horizontal flex row — FrequencyStrip (left) + KnobStrip panel (right); gap-4 between them

KnobStrip: vertical card with three RotaryKnob components side by side — Frequency (Hz, logarithmic=true, min=20, max=20000), Gain (dB, linear, min=-gainRange, max=gainRange), Q (linear, min=qMin, max=qMax) — ranges from current difficulty config
FrequencyIdEngine is instantiated once; load('/audio/freq-id-placeholder.wav') called on component mount (browser only)
setFilter() called with the current round's targetFreq, targetGainDb, targetQ when round starts — this applies the hidden EQ
Audio autoplays in B mode when round starts (play('B') called after load resolves)

ABToggle component (or equivalent two-button toggle): A and B buttons side by side; active button is bg-primary text-primary-foreground; inactive is bg-muted text-muted-foreground; clicking calls engine.setMode()
$state variable freqKnobMoved: boolean (false by default); set to true on first frequency knob onchange
Submit button: disabled (and visually dimmed) until freqKnobMoved is true
On submit: call evaluateGuess(currentRound, userFreq), update round.result and round.userFreq/userGainDb/userQ, update session.score if correct, set gameState to 'round_result'
Read tasks/design-frequency-id.md RoundUI section before implementing
Use Svelte MCP (list-sections → get-documentation) before writing any .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on every .svelte file and fix all reported issues
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)
Lint passes (pnpm lint)
Verify in browser: round UI shows with knobs, A/B toggle, frequency strip; Submit disabled until freq knob moved; A/B switch toggles audio mode

userStories.title: Round result + game over screens
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback after each guess and a final score screen after 5 rounds so I can learn from my answers and choose to continue.
acceptanceCriteria: Round result view renders when gameState === 'round_result'
Shows 'Correct ✓' or 'Incorrect ✗' in large text (text-2xl font-semibold); color: text-foreground for correct, text-destructive for incorrect
Shows target values: target freq (formatted e.g. '1.2 kHz'), target gain (e.g. '+5 dB'), target Q (e.g. '1.5')
Shows user's guess values side-by-side with targets for comparison
Shows the allowed error margin (e.g. '±15% tolerance')
FrequencyStrip shown with showTarget=true — both user marker and target marker visible
'Next Round' button if currentRoundIndex < 4; 'See Results' button on round 5 (index 4)

Clicking Next Round: increments currentRoundIndex, resets freqKnobMoved to false, resets knob values to center defaults, calls engine.stop() then play('B') with new round's filter, sets gameState to 'round_active'
Clicking See Results: sets gameState to 'game_over'
Game over view renders when gameState === 'game_over'
Score displayed as 'X / 5' (text-4xl font-mono)
List of all 5 rounds: round number, target freq (formatted), user guess freq, correct/incorrect badge
'Play Again' button: calls createGameSession(same difficulty), resets to round 1, sets gameState to 'round_active' (skips difficulty selection)
'Back to Home' button: navigates to /
Read tasks/design-frequency-id.md RoundResult and GameOver sections before implementing
Use Svelte MCP (list-sections → get-documentation) before writing any .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on every .svelte file and fix all reported issues
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)
Lint passes (pnpm lint)
Verify in browser: complete a round — result screen shows; complete all 5 — game over shows with score and round list

userStories.title: localStorage stats persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Frequency ID game results saved to localStorage so they appear in the Auris dashboard stats.
acceptanceCriteria: On game over (when gameState transitions to 'game_over'), append a FrequencyIdResult entry to localStorage key 'auris_freq_id_history'
FrequencyIdResult shape: { timestamp: string (ISO), difficulty: Difficulty, score: number, rounds: Round[] }
Reading and writing 'auris_freq_id_history' is SSR-safe (guard with browser from $app/environment)
src/lib/stats.ts updated: AurisStats type gains two new fields: freqIdGamesPlayed: number, freqIdBestScore: number | null
getStats() reads 'auris_freq_id_history' and computes freqIdGamesPlayed (length of array) and freqIdBestScore (max score, or null if no games)
saveStats() signature unchanged — freqIdGamesPlayed and freqIdBestScore are derived, not stored in 'auris:stats'
Dashboard stats panel (src/routes/+page.svelte or src/lib/components/stats-panel.svelte) updated to display freqIdGamesPlayed and freqIdBestScore with labels 'FREQ ID GAMES' and 'FREQ ID BEST'
Empty state for both new fields shows '—' when no games played
Frequency ID game card in dashboard GAMES array updated to available: true with href: '/games/frequency-id'
Typecheck passes (pnpm check)
Lint passes (pnpm lint)
